channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democratic lawmakers from Connecticut report Thanksgiving bomb threats against their homes
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — At least five Democratic members of Congress from Connecticut were targeted by bomb threats on their homes Thursday, the lawmakers or their offices said. Sen. Chris Murphy and Reps.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Texas could bus migrants directly to ICE for deportation instead of sanctuary cities under proposed plan
item.description: Texas could bus migrants to ICE instead of sanctuary cities so they can be processed for deportation faster under a proposed plan, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top 5 biggest political turkeys ripped by conservatives in 2024
item.description: Prominent Democrats experienced a disappointing 2024 across the political spectrum after President-elect Trump returned to the White House after a contentious election cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is Evacuation Day? The forgotten holiday that predates Thanksgiving
item.description: Before Thanksgiving became the prominent late November holiday, much of America celebrated "Evacuation Day" – commemorating the day the last Redcoats fled New York in 1783.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump dined on Wednesday with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, bringing together the Facebook founder and the former president who was once banned from that social network.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump claims a win on immigration after a call with Mexico's president. But she suggests no change
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is declaring a win on stopping illegal immigration through Mexico after talking with that country’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Mexico will stop flow of migrants after speaking with Mexican president following tariff threats
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday said he had a productive conversation with Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo over the phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump turns to outsider to shake up Navy, but his lack of military experience raises concerns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump's nominee to be secretary of the Navy, John Phelan, has not served in the military or had a civilian leadership role in the service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrat Derek Tran unseats Rep. Michelle Steel in California's 45th Congressional District
item.description: U.S. Army Veteran Derek Tran has won California 45th Congressional District, unseating Rep. Michelle Streel in one of the state's most closely-watched races.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeWine signs bill restricting bathroom access for transgender Ohio students
item.description: Transgender students may use single-occupancy or faculty facilities with “controlled use,” according to the bill, Senate Bill 104.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Meet the medical contrarians picked to lead health agencies under Trump and Kennedy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has assembled a team of medical contrarians and health care critics to fulfill an agenda aimed at remaking how the federal government oversees medicines, health programs and nutrition. On Tuesday night, Trump nominated Dr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scott County official says 20 absentee ballots in close House race were likely tossed
item.description: The missing ballots are at the heart of a court challenge from Minnesota House Republicans who have said a 14-vote win for a DFLer there is clouded. The race will dictate House control.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates former Pence military aide for new Ukraine post
item.description: Former Pence national security adviser Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg is reportedly favored for a Ukraine envoy spot in the new Trump administration, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Carolyn Goodman prepares to leave office, ending an era
item.description: Carolyn Goodman, who has served as Mayor of Las Vegas for 13 years, was honored with the renaming of Civic Plaza in her honor, and looks forward to the future with her husband Oscar.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: (The Hill) -- Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia expels two German journalists in tit-for-tat retaliation, ministry says
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's Foreign Ministry said Wednesday it has revoked the accreditation of two employees with the German ARD broadcaster and ordered them to leave the country in what it described as a retaliation to German authorities' move targeting two journalists from Russia's state TV.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cuomo joins Netanyahu’s legal defense team against ICC warrants as he mulls 2025 NYC mayoral run
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew M. Cuomo joined Bibi Netanyahu's legal defense team this week, as the Israeli leader faces war crimes charges from the ICC.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House pressing Ukraine to draft 18-year-olds so it has enough troops to battle Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden’s administration is urging Ukraine to quickly increase the size of its military by drafting more troops and revamping its mobilization laws to allow for the conscription of those as young as 18.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US sanctions more allies of Maduro over alleged post-election crackdown in Venezuela
item.description: The United States has imposed sanctions on an additional 21 allies of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, accusing them of supporting and carrying out acts of repression following July's disputed presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawmakers call for progress on marijuana law before end of Biden administration
item.description: Progressive lawmakers are calling on President Biden to take additional steps on federal marijuana law before his term comes to an end in January. In a letter sent to Biden and Vice President Harris, Democratic Reps. Barbara Lee (Calif.), Earl Blumenauer (Ore.) and Ilhan Omar (Minn.) and Sens.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Jay Bhattacharya, who backed COVID herd immunity, to lead National Institutes of Health
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen health economist Dr. Jay Bhattacharya, a critic of pandemic lockdowns and vaccine mandates, to lead the National Institutes of Health, the nation's leading medical research agency.
item.dc:creator: CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mississippi runoff election for state Supreme Court justice is too close to call
item.description: A runoff election in the state Supreme Court in Mississippi race is too close to call, with State Sen. Jenifer Branning and Justice Jim Kitchens neck and neck as of Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: End of Trump prosecutions renews scrutiny of DOJ's pace
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith's move to dismiss Donald Trump's two federal indictments has sparked finger-pointing from those eager to see the president-elect held to account, with critics airing their frustrations at the Department of Justice (DOJ) and the courts for the anticlimactic end to the case.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 4 takeaways from Trump's health agency nominations
item.description: Almost all have been highly critical of the agencies they would run if confirmed, and experts say the nominees represent a complete overhaul of the vision and priorities of key agencies for health care and public health.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names speechwriter Vince Haley as Domestic Policy Council chief
item.description: President-elect Trump announced on Tuesday night that Vince Haley, his campaign speechwriter, will be the next chief of the Domestic Policy Council (DPC). “As Director of the Domestic Policy Council, Vince will help make life better for ALL Americans, and unify our Country through SUCCESS.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump turns to critic of COVID mandates to run NIH
item.description: Dr. Jay Bhattacharya, a Stanford health researcher, is in line to lead the National Institutes of Health. Early in the pandemic he argued against lockdowns and focusing on people at highest risk.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: President Trump endorses Patronis & Fine for congressional seats
item.description: Two Florida leaders are already emerging as potential frontrunners to replace Former Congressman Matt Gaetz and current U.S. Representative Mike Waltz in D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After delay, Trump signs agreement with Biden White House to begin formal transition handoff
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday reached a required agreement with President Joe Biden’s White House to allow his transition staff to coordinate with the existing federal workforce before taking office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cheap Ozempic? How millions of Americans with obesity may get access to costly weight-loss drugs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Millions of obese Americans would get access to popular weekly injectables that would help them shed pounds quickly if a $ 35 billion proposal from the Biden administration is blessed by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's tariffs may be targeted at others — but they could impact you too
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump threatened to slap hefty tariffs on China, Canada and Mexico. Those tariffs would hurt America too — if they are carried out.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: State bar counsel filed 2 complaints against Clark County Commissioner Justin Jones
item.description: The office of the state bar counsel, in its effort to discipline Clark County Commissioner Justin Jones, filed a complaint in February 2024 and another in October where it alleges Jones violated several rules of professional conduct, according to documents the 8 News Now Investigators have obtained.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rand Paul: Denver mayor could be removed over deportation resistance
item.description: Denver Mayor Mike Johnston (D) has vowed to protect migrants in his community as the Trump transition team promises it will crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens Canada, Mexico, China with tariffs
item.description: He said the countries weren't doing enough to control the flow of illegal drugs, especially fentanyl, and migrants across the border.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Special counsel moves to dismiss election interference case against President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith asked a federal judge to dismiss the case accusing President-elect Donald Trump of plotting to overturn the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
